Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 1997 | Question: 172

Answer the following question based on the information given below.

The bar chart shows the revenue received, in million US Dollars (USD), from subscribers to a particular Internet service. The data covers the period \( 2003-2007 \) for the Unites States (US) and Europe. The bar chart also shows the estimated revenues from subscription to this service for the period \( 2008 – 2010. \)

The difference between the estimated subscription in Europe in \( 2008 \) and what it would have been if it were computed using the percentage growth rate of \( 2007\; ( \)over \( 2006), \) is closest to:



Quantitative Aptitude: CAT 2008 | Question: 37

A student walks to the bus stop to catch a bus to the university. He then walks from the bus stop at the university to the students union arriving there at \( 8:35 \; \text{am.} \)

 

How far does the student walk in total?

  1. \( 1 \; \text{Km} \)
  2. \( 2 \; \text{Km} \)
  3. \( 3 \; \text{Km} \)
  4. \( 4 \; \text{Km} \)
  5. \( 5 \; \text{Km} \)

How far is he from university student's union at \( 8:20 \; \text{am}? \)

  1. \( 1 \; \text{Km} \)
  2. \( 2 \; \text{Km} \)
  3. \( 3 \; \text{Km} \)
  4. \( 4 \; \text{Km} \)
  5. \( 5 \; \text{Km} \)

What is the average speed of the bus?

  1. \( 14 \; \text{Kmph} \)
  2. \( 24 \; \text{Kmph} \)
  3. \( 40 \; \text{Kmph} \)
  4. \( 48 \; \text{Kmph} \)


Quantitative Aptitude: How far does the student walk in total?

If between 1987 and 2007 the trend for fashion ties had been the same as for cravats, how many fashion ties would have been sold in 2007?

  1. 72600
  2. 72100
  3. 71300
  4. 70500
  5. 69200

For all types of tie together, what is the percentage decline between 2002 and 2007? (to nearest five percent. Clip ons and Cravats are types of tie)

  1. 15%
  2. 20%
  3. 25%
  4. 30%
  5. 35%

What is the approximate absolute change in percentage market share for fashion tie between 1987 and 2007?

  1. 15%
  2. 20%
  3. 25%
  4. 30%
  5. 35%

If the average retail price for silk ties in 2002 was £ 17.50 plus value added Tax at 17.5% what was the total value of silk Ties sold in 2002? ( to nearest £1000)

  1. £372,000
  2. £382,000
  3. £392,000
  4. £402,000
  5. £412.000

 

 



Quantitative Aptitude: If between 1987 and 2007 the trend for fashion ties had been the same as for cravats, how many fashion ties would have been sold in 2007?

What was the largest number of students in any year that went on to further study?

  1. 561
  2. 576
  3. 585
  4. 592
  5. Can't Say 

What was the decrease in the number of graduates in employment between 2002 and 2004?

  1. 125
  2. 135
  3. 140
  4. 180
  5. Can't Say

In 2004 how many social science students were in employment after graduating?

  1. 260
  2. 272
  3. 284
  4. 290
  5. Can't Say


Quantitative Aptitude: What was the largest number of students in any year that went on to further study?

What was the population of Botsibia (millions) in 2006?

  1. 14.5
  2. 15.5
  3. 16.5
  4. 17.5
  5. 18.5

How many million cars were there in Korgolia in 2001?

  1. 9.2
  2. 9.7
  3. 10.2
  4. 10.7
  5. 11.2

Which country had the greatest percentage change in the numbers of cars per person between 2001 and 2006?

  1. Normark
  2. Serbistan
  3. Botsibia
  4. Velumbia
  5. Korgolia

If in 2001 the ratio of cars per person in Velumbia had been the same as that of Normark, how many cars would there be in Velumbia?

  1. 6.65M
  2. 6.75M
  3. 6.85M
  4. 6.95M
  5. 7.05M


Quantitative Aptitude: What was the population of Botsibia (millions) in 2006 ?

Which company had the widest range of share prices over the 5 years?

  1. Pilemin Airlines
  2. RentaBanga
  3. Rupt Bank
  4. Cash Casinos
  5. Stackem Supermarts
Which year had the widest range of Share prices?
  1. 2002
  2. 2003
  3. 2004
  4. 2005
  5. 2006
Between 2002 and 2006 what was the approximate total change in share prices for all companies?
  1. -15%
  2. -5%
  3. 5%
  4. +15%
  5. +25%
If the proportional change in share price for pilemin Airlines had been the same as that for Rupt bank between 2002 and 2006 what would have been the value of Pilemin Airlines shares in 2006?
  1. 3.31
  2. 3.81
  3. 4.31
  4. 4.81
  5. 5.31
If in 2006-07, the average share price changes by the same percentage as the percentage of average share price increase between 2002 and 2003 for all these companies, what will be the change in average share price for all these companies between 2006 & 2007?
  1. 11 p
  2. 15 p
  3. 19 p
  4. 23 p
  5. 27 p
 

 



Quantitative Aptitude: Which company had the widest range of share prices over the 5 years?

Which company's sales were most consistent throughout the year? 

  1. Hole in Roof
  2. Hot House
  3. Stone's Throw
  4. Leaky Windows
  5. Frying Tonight

In which month did the sales of Hole in Roof and Stones' Throw differ the most?

  1. January
  2. March
  3. July
  4. August
  5. October

Which month gave the largest number of sales for all the companies combined?

  1. April
  2. May
  3. June
  4. July
  5. August

What was the percentage increase in sales for Hot House between July and August?

  1. 20
  2. 30
  3. 40
  4. 50
  5. 60


Quantitative Aptitude: Which company's sales were most consistent throughout the year?

The graph above gives the number of computers sold each month (in thousands) by three different computer Manufacturer \( 1 \) (in red), Manufacturer \( 2 \) (in blue) and Manufacturer \( 3 \) (in yellow).

Which month showed the largest total decrease in PC sales over the previous month?

  1. March
  2. April
  3. May 
  4. June 
  5. July

What percentage of manufacturer 2' s sales were made in April ( to the nearest percent)

  1. 16
  2. 22
  3. 27
  4. 33
  5. 38

If the average profit made on each PC sold by manufacturer 3 over all 5 months was 78 pounds what was the total profit in pounds on all sales in this period by that manufacturer?

  1. 650,400
  2. 820,700
  3. 980,300
  4. 1,095,600
  5. 1,177,800


Quantitative Aptitude: Which month showed the largest total decrease in PC sales over the previous month?
